rag_rules: Fix type mismatches in AGREE expressions by ensuring operand types match (e.g., real * real, real - real).
Resolve 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors by tracing unresolved identifiers or invalid helpers on the same line.
Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes; move valid assigns to implementation scope.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE annexes.
Ensure AGREE scalar types are declared as int, real, or bool.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless model exposes typed ports or constants.
When AGREE diagnostics point to invalid references, delete or rewrite clauses using only visible identifiers.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type contracts should reference only visible ports and features.
Assignments in AGREE must map outputs to valid expressions in their scope.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to fix type errors by introducing new identifiers or types not present in the AADL model.
Do not place implementation-level guarantees or assignments in component type annexes.
Do not assume AADL property names like 'Period' or 'Deadline' are automatically available in AGREE scope.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE validator diagnostics beyond type checking
Model fusion and component ownership rules
